title: Eclipse-class dreadnought

{{Quote|Wonderful name it has— the ''Eclipse''. It certainly blocked out my view of the shipyards.|[[Tyber Zann]]|<ref name="Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption">''[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption]]''</ref>}}
The '''''Eclipse''-class dreadnought''', also known as the '''''Eclipse''-class Super Star Destroyer''', was a class of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] manufactured by [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]] and mainly used around [[10 ABY/Legends|six years after]] the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. Like the [[Sovereign-class Super Star Destroyer|''Sovereign''-class]], they were regarded as a new generation of Super Star Destroyer.

==Characteristics==
===Size===
The massive ''Eclipse''-class vessels measured 17.5 [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] in length, and was one of the largest ships to ever be constructed.<ref name="DESB" /> Although the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] measured 1.5 kilometers more in length, the ''Eclipse''-class had a far greater mass than any other [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]].<ref name="SOTGSE" />

===Offensive and defensive systems===
[[File:Eclipsepro.jpg|thumb|left|150px|[[Eclipse (Eclipse-class)|''Eclipse'']] under construction.]]
The most deadly weapon carried by ''Eclipse''-class ships was an [[Axial superlaser/Legends|axial superlaser]], similar to but significantly less powerful than one of the component beams used in the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star's]] [[Concave Dish Composite Beam Superlaser|superlaser]].<ref name="DESB" />

An ''Eclipse''{{'s}} superlaser easily had the power to destroy other Super Star Destroyers, as the [[Annihilator (Executor-class)/Legends|''Annihilator'']] found out when [[Tyber Zann]] seized the ''Eclipse''{{'s}} incredible power above Kuat.<ref name="Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption" />

The axial superlasers were capable of slashing through any [[Planetary shield/Legends|planetary shield]] to crack open a planet's crust and sear entire continental landmasses.<ref name="DESB" /><ref name="TNEGTVV" /> The massive superlaser was more widely used to destroy enemy capital ships and space stations during combat.<ref name="Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption" /> According to [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Conan Antonio Motti/Legends|Conan Antonio Motti]] in the ''[[Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]'', the superlaser's intended use was to penetrate planetary shields as well as vaporize cities with a single blast.<ref name="Handbook" />

The class benefited from the most significant weaponry-improvements of the last few decades. This included [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projectors]] and improved [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]],<ref name="DESB" /> the former of which were the same types included on the [[Interdictor-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Interdictor''-class Star Destroyers]].<ref name="Handbook" /> Additionally, these behemoths were armed with hundreds of heavy laser and [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] mountings.<ref name="DESB" />

In addition to its firepower, the ''Eclipse''-class's hull armor and shields were so strong that it could ram enemy ships without hesitation.<ref name="TNEGTVV" /><ref name="EGVV">''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]''</ref>

===Propulsion systems===
The ''Eclipse''-class was more mobile than the Death Star, being equipped with both an enhanced [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] and fast [[Sublight engine/Legends|sublight engines]].<ref name="DESB" />

===Complement===
An ''Eclipse''-class vessel carried 600 [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE/IN interceptors]] and 96 [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE Bombers]], divided into 58 [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]]; for ground assault, they carried 150,000 Imperial soldiers, 100 [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-ATs]], and five [[IM-455 modular garrison|prefabricated bases]].<ref name="DESB" /> The hangars were located at the sides of the vessel, with the fore and aft launch bays closely spaced underneath this area, towards the middle of the ship.<ref name="EGVV" />

Each ship of the class was crewed by over 700,000 personnel,<ref name="DESB" /> and ''[[Eclipse II]]'' also carried a complement of [[Shadow Droid]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]].<ref name="Empire's End">''[[Star Wars: Empire's End]]''</ref> Troop quarters were located on the lower frontal section of the vessel, in front of the hangar areas.<ref name="EGVV" />

===Bridge tower===
The bridge tower contained the main communications array and sensor array, located on the port and starboard sides, respectively. In addition to containing the main command station for the ship on the lower portion of the tower, it also housed the deflector shield command center, on the top area.<ref name="EGVV" /> The bridge tower was closer to the hull than that of traditional Star Destroyers of the ''Imperial-I'' and ''Imperial-II'' class.

===Variances===
There were several variances between the two known vessels of this class, the [[Eclipse (Eclipse-class)|''Eclipse I'']] and the ''[[Eclipse II]]'': The ''Eclipse I'' possessed six engines on the back, whereas the ''Eclipse II'' possessed eight. In addition, the ''Eclipse I'' had the engines separate from each other and to the sides, whereas the ''Eclipse II'' engines were grouped together in a manner similar to other Star Destroyers. Lastly, the ''Eclipse II'' had a command bridge tower directly over the superlaser weapon that the ''Eclipse I'' lacked.

==History==
{{Quote|The hyperspace tunnel opened, and this black thing came crawling out from between the stars… We thought it was a ghost ship, until it opened fire.|[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] information officer [[Nara Dun]]|<ref name="TNEGTVV" />}}
[[File:Eclipse1.jpg|thumb|left|230px|The ''Eclipse'' enters the [[Da Soocha V|Pinnacle Moon]] system, escorted by two [[Allegiance-class battlecruiser|''Allegiance''-class battlecruisers]] and a third ship.]]
While the lead ship, the [[Eclipse (Eclipse-class)|''Eclipse'']], was being built, the Empire also constructed an ''Eclipse''-class prototype to use as a weapons testbed. This craft was later converted to become the [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] [[Tarkin (superweapon)|''Tarkin'']].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> The ''Tarkin'' was eventually destroyed by a Rebel sabotage team in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].<ref name="SW52">[[Star Wars (1977) 52|''Star Wars'' (1977) 52]]</ref>

The ''Eclipse'', alongside its sister ship, the ''[[Eclipse II]]'', began construction early in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], around [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]].<ref name="Underworld: A Galaxy of Scum and Villainy">{{InsiderCite|89|Underworld: A Galaxy of Scum and Villainy}}</ref> The ship class, alongside that of a similar Star Dreadnought class called the [[Sovereign-class Super Star Destroyer|''Sovereign''-class]], were planned to revolutionize [[Space warfare/Legends|naval combat]] as well as ensure the dominance of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] up to the next century of its existence. Details of the planned design were included in the [[Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide|''Imperial Handbook'']], an official ''aide-memoire'' for Imperial [[Commander/Legends|commanders]].<ref name="Handbook" /> The ''Eclipse'' was built above Kuat, whereas the ''Eclipse II'' was built over Byss. The appearance of both ships' hulls was derived from ancient nautical warships.<ref name="EGVV" />

By [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], both the ''Eclipse'' and the ''Eclipse II'' had its superlaser main weapon installed and in functional condition. However, both ships ended up significantly delayed in terms of completion thanks to Palpatine's [[Battle of Endor/Legends|demise]] and the power vacuum that ensued. The then-fledgling [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] remained unaware of the ''Eclipse''-class' construction: though the [[New Republic Intelligence Service]] acquired a copy of the ''Imperial Handbook'' just after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], this was the only information they received on the class and [[General/Legends|General]] [[Carlist Rieekan/Legends|Carlist Rieekan]] believed that the Empire had not made progress on the design.<ref name="Handbook" /> In addition, the [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Tyber Zann]] wanted to access records on the ship, and if possible steal the ship, so he [[Battle for the Eclipse|attacked]] Kuat and took control of the ship, and used it to damage an Imperial and rebel fleet (with his manipulating the latter group into granting him access). Afterwards, Zann had no further use for the vessel, finding its size to be more cumbersome for laying low to even the Rebel Alliance, and left it adrift, until the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] attacked and destroyed the ''Eclipse''<ref name="Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption" />

Later on, this ship was completed and became the reborn [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] flagship, with its sister ship ''[[Eclipse II]]'' assuming the same role after the original's destruction.<ref name="Empire's End" />

Approximately 17.5 kilometers long on the keel, these jet-black battleships were among the largest [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyers]] ever built, and among the most heavily armed warships of all time. In terms of scale, they were the successors to dreadnoughts like ''[[Eye of Palpatine]]''<ref name="Children of the Jedi">[[Children of the Jedi (novel)|''Children of the Jedi'']]</ref> and the Kuati Star Dreadnoughts that preceded the [[New Republic era]].<ref name="Attack of the Clones: Incredible Cross-Sections">''[[Star Wars: Attack of the Clones Incredible Cross-Sections]]''</ref><ref name="Star Wars: Complete Locations">[[Star Wars: Complete Locations (2005)|''Star Wars: Complete Locations'']]</ref>

''Eclipse'' took almost as long to construct as either of the Empire's Death Star battlemoons, and for most of her career, she served as an orbital battlestation at [[Byss/Legends|Byss]].<ref name="DESB" /><ref name="Byss and the Deep Core">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050414planet2|series=Byss and the Deep Core|text=Part 2: Empress Teta}}</ref> At some point later, the ''Eclipse'' came out of hyperspace, to which its black hull caused some New Republic forces to initially believe that it was a ghost ship, although they quickly realized that the ship was in fact real when it opened fire to which only the information officer, [[Nara Dun]], survived and managed to inform New Republic forces about the devastating surprise attack.<ref name="TNEGTVV" />

She was destroyed along with the reborn Emperor in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], during an Imperial attack on [[Da Soocha V|Pinnacle Moon]] in the [[Da Soocha]] system, when [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and his sister, [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]], managed to disrupt the Emperor's control of a titanic [[Force storm (wormhole)|Force storm]], a massive conflagration of [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] energy that the Emperor had summoned in order to wipe out the massed [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] fleet.<ref name="Dark Empire">''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]''</ref>

After this catastrophe, the ''Eclipse II'' replaced the original. It was slightly different than its predecessor, with different thrusters. Above the planet [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]], [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] and [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] led a small boarding party against the mighty battleship, and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] was able to override ''Eclipse II''{{'s}} computer banks. The [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] then sent ''Eclipse II'' hurtling through hyperspace on a collision course with the superweapon [[Galaxy Gun]]. ''Eclipse II'' rammed the Galaxy Gun, destroying both. One last projectile was pulled by Byss' gravity and impacted the planet, the resulting explosion [[Destruction of Byss|annihilating]] Byss and the clones of Emperor Palpatine.<ref name="Empire's End" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Eclipse-class_Star_Destroyer1.jpg|thumb|left|230px|An ''Eclipse''-class [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] with a [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] to scale.]]
The ''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' states that ''Eclipse'' was 17.5 kilometers long; this contradicts the description given in ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]'', where the ship is said to reach "ten miles from stem to stern", or 16 kilometers. The author of the ''Dark Empire Sourcebook'' has apparently suggested that an additional 1.5 kilometers was added to the first ship of this class. This is supposed to indicate that particular ship's customization and personalization, with the standard design being only 16 kilometers long. Alternatively, "ten miles from stem to stern" might be merely a vague, rhetorical figure, or the discrepancy may be the result of the long and spasmodic construction period, or it may represent a difference between ''Eclipse'' and ''Eclipse II''. To add to the confusion, the ''Eclipse'' entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' states a length of 17.5 kilometers, where the Star Destroyer entry has the length at 16 kilometers, which could be a typo since the Star Destroyer is 1,600 meters long.

Regardless of size, considering the initial statements of the size of [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnoughts]], it's clear that the Eclipse was intended to be much larger than them. With later statements about the size of Executor ships, the Eclipse is actually shorter, though still likely more massive and more destructive.

There is also an apparent contradiction in the information on ''Eclipse''{{'s}} construction. According to the ''Dark Empire Sourcebook'', design work on ''Eclipse'' was begun after the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], while ''[[Byss and the Deep Core]]'' claims that she was under construction for as long as either Death Star, hinting a timeframe of decades (it should be noted that, at the time ''Byss and the Deep Core'' was written, the timescale of Death Star construction was believed to be much shorter, on the order of years).

''Byss and the Deep Core'' further hints that ''Eclipse'' was parked in orbit at Byss before the Battle of Endor, as it is only in this timeframe that she could have served as a rendezvous point for meetings of the [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admirals]] as claimed in the text (although the Grand Admirals referred to may be a new group that postdates the original 12); but [[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] says that she was built at [[Kuat/Legends|Kuat]] and only moved to Byss four and a half years after the Battle of Endor, when she was stolen in a partially-completed state by [[Lira Wessex/Legends|Lira Wessex]] and her design team shortly before the shipyards surrendered to the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].

There are several ways of explaining these perceived discrepancies, but it is possible that, as with [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']] and her sister-ship ''[[Lusankya/Legends|Lusankya]]'' (also originally named ''Executor''), ''Eclipse'' and ''Eclipse II'' were constructed simultaneously in two different shipyards, with the existence of each being kept secret from the people working on the other. The potential discrepancies in timeframe may be a matter of in-universe error, with the fictional authors who serve as the real-world writers' proxies being unaware of the true length of construction.

[[File:Esuper_laser2.jpg|thumb|right|230px|[[Eclipse (Eclipse-class)|''Eclipse'']] fires its [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]], destroying a [[MC80 Liberty Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|MC80 ''Liberty''-type Star Cruiser]].]]
Some fans regard the stated power of ''Eclipse''{{'s}} superlaser as paradoxical. It is described as not powerful enough to destroy a planet, but as capable of overpowering planetary shields and as 2/3rds the power of the Death Star's prime weapon; but some fan calculations suggest that a weapon of such power would be more than sufficient to destroy a planet, and some fans believe that planetary shields are actually harder to destroy than planets. Those who wish to reconcile these beliefs with the canon statements suggest that the "2/3rds" figure refers instead to the magnitude of the energy release as opposed to the total value; this would put the main weapon below the threshold necessary to destroy a planet.

Another possibility, as the Death Star's superlaser consisted of eight individual beams which combined into a single larger beam after firing, is that the ''Eclipse''-class' superlaser had 2/3rds the power of ''one'' of the Death Star superlaser's component beams. Alternatively, it may be that the fan-calculations are in error; the exact destructive mechanism of a hypermatter-fueled superlaser isn't fully understood other than it being a direct energy transfer weapon. With ''The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels'' mentioning it as being made to penetrate a planet's crust, this is still far less than what either Death Star was capable of, which was pulverizing an entire planet.

Another suggestion is that perhaps ''Eclipse'' could destroy a planet, but not if the shot's power is depleted by having to travel through a planetary shield.
